title: manulea aethiopica
intro: Manulea aethiopica, also known as the Red Honeysuckle or Red Coral Honeysuckle, is a flowering plant native to South Africa. It is a shrub-like plant with red flowers and green leaves, traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant's health benefits include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Manulea aethiopica can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, typically by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, to utilize its medicinal properties.
